title: ECTROPY NEXT PHASE HANDOFF: Prisma Models, Graph Migration & SEPPA MCP Operationalization

metadata.title: ECTROPY NEXT PHASE HANDOFF: Prisma Models, Graph Migration & SEPPA MCP Operationalization

content.raw:
# ECTROPY NEXT PHASE HANDOFF: Prisma Models, Graph Migration & SEPPA MCP Operationalization

**Document ID:** HANDOFF-PRISMA-GRAPH-SEPPA-2026-01-16  
**Status:** Ready for Implementation  
**Priority:** P1 - Critical Path to Canadian Pilot (March 2026)  
**Estimated Effort:** 280-340 hours across 3 workstreams

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This handoff document provides everything needed to continue Ectropy development across three interconnected workstreams:

1. **Prisma Model Extension** - Add 15 Contract-to-Operations models to existing schema
2. **Graph Migration Preparation** - Dual-write architecture for PostgreSQL → Neo4j
3. **SEPPA MCP Operationalization** - Register 22 new tools, connect to Claude assistant

**Critical Dependencies:**

- Phase 3-4 Contract-to-Operations documentation is COMPLETE (39 MCP tools specified)
- SEPPA backend (Phase 3) is COMPLETE with 24 tools registered
- All V3 JSON schemas exist in `.roadmap/schemas/`
- Repository split scheduled for January 17, 2026

---

## PART 1: CURRENT STATE SUMMARY

### 1.1 Documentation Complete (Just Finished)

| Artifact                    | Location                                                        | Status          |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- |
| Contract schemas (3)        | `.roadmap/schemas/contract/`                                    | ✅ V3 compliant |
| IPD schemas (3)             | `.roadmap/schemas/ipd/`                                         | ✅ V3 compliant |
| Closeout schemas (3)        | `.roadmap/schemas/closeout/`                                    | ✅ V3 compliant |
| Commissioning schemas (3)   | `.roadmap/schemas/commissioning/`                               | ✅ V3 compliant |
| Warranty schemas (3)        | `.roadmap/schemas/warranty/`                                    | ✅ V3 compliant |
| Feature FEATURE.json (6)    | `.roadmap/features/*/FEATURE.json`                              | ✅ Complete     |
| Feature interfaces.json (6) | `.roadmap/features/*/interfaces.json`                           | ✅ Complete     |
| Completion report           | `.roadmap/features/CONTRACT_TO_OPERATIONS_COMPLETION_REPORT.md` | ✅ Created      |
| Strategic analysis          | `.roadmap/features/STRATEGIC_TECHNICAL_ANALYSIS_2026-01-16.md`  | ✅ Created      |

### 1.2 Prisma Models Current State (12 Models)

```
prisma/schema.prisma
├── AuthorityLevel         ✅ 7-tier cascade definition
├── Participant            ✅ Project stakeholder
├── Voxel                  ✅ 3D spatial decision container
├── PMDecision             ✅ Construction decision
├── VoxelDecisionAttachment ✅ Decision-to-voxel link
├── ToleranceOverride      ✅ Pre-approved variance
├── PreApproval            ✅ Standing approval scope
├── VoxelAlert             ✅ Spatial alert
├── Acknowledgment         ✅ Worker acknowledgment
├── Consequence            ✅ Decision outcome tracking
├── ScheduleProposal       ✅ Look-ahead modification
└── Inspection             ✅ Field inspection record
```

### 1.3 MCP Tools Current State (39 Total: 17 Implemented + 22 Specified)

**Implemented in `mcp-server` (17 tools):**

```
Decision Management:    create_decision_request, analyze_decision_authority,
                       record_decision, create_decision_template

Graph Operations:       traverse_decision_graph, attach_decision_to_voxels,
                       get_voxel_decision_surface

Voxel Operations:       acknowledge_voxel_decision, find_voxels_for_work,
                       create_tolerance_override, calculate_voxel_savings

Schedule Integration:   create_schedule_proposal, get_10day_lookahead

Consequence Tracking:   record_consequence

Inspection Integration: get_inspection_view, record_inspection

Community:              calculate_reward
```

**Registered in SEPPA tool-registry.ts (24 total):**

- 21 PM Decision tools (from `pm-decision-tools.ts`)
- 3 Core tools (get_project_status, search_decisions, get_user_pending_actions)

**Specified but NOT implemented (22 tools):**

```
Contract Management (3):     parse_contract_tool, extract_authority_cascade_tool,
                            configure_project_from_contract_tool

IPD Governance (6):          initiate_pmt_vote_tool, cast_vote_tool, get_voting_status_tool,
                            escalate_to_pet_tool, update_target_cost_tool,
                            calculate_savings_projection_tool

Closeout (3):                initiate_closeout_sequence_tool, generate_substantial_completion_tool,
                            generate_final_accounting_tool

Commissioning (3):           log_fpt_result_tool, link_cx_issue_to_decision_tool,
                            generate_cx_documentation_tool

Punch List (4):              create_punch_item_tool, update_punch_item_tool,
                            assign_punch_item_tool, validate_punch_completion_tool

Warranty/Operations (3):     create_warranty_claim_tool, search_decision_archive_tool,
                            query_building_history_tool
```

### 1.4 SEPPA Backend State (Phase 3 Complete)

```
apps/mcp-server/src/services/assistant/
├── index.ts              ✅ Barrel exports
├── types.ts              ✅ Core interfaces, authority types
├── claude-client.ts      ✅ Anthropic SDK wrapper (claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
├── tool-registry.ts      ✅ 24 tools registered
├── tool-executor.ts      ✅ Bridges Claude tool_use to MCP handlers
├── system-prompt.ts      ✅ Dynamic prompt with authority injection
├── conversation-store.ts ✅ In-memory conversation storage
├── context-builder.ts    ✅ Message history and context enrichment
└── assistant.service.ts  ✅ Main orchestration with tool loop

REST API Endpoints:
├── POST /api/assistant/chat           ✅ Send message, get response
├── GET  /api/assistant/conversations/:id ✅ Retrieve conversation
├── DELETE /api/assistant/conversations/:id ✅ Delete conversation
└── POST /api/assistant/conversations/:id/context ✅ Update context
```

## PART 2: WORKSTREAM A - PRISMA MODEL EXTENSION

### 2.1 Models to Create (15 New Models)

**Source Schema → Prisma Model Mapping:**

| JSON Schema                        | Prisma Model          | Domain        | Priority |
| ---------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------- | -------- |
| `contract.schema.json`             | `Contract`            | Contract      | P1       |
| `contract-party.schema.json`       | `ContractParty`       | Contract      | P1       |
| `contract-template.schema.json`    | `ContractTemplate`    | Contract      | P2       |
| `voting-session.schema.json`       | `VotingSession`       | IPD           | P2       |
| `target-cost.schema.json`          | `TargetCost`          | IPD           | P2       |
| `savings-distribution.schema.json` | `SavingsDistribution` | IPD           | P2       |
| `closeout.schema.json`             | `Closeout`            | Closeout      | P2       |
| `g704-certificate.schema.json`     | `G704Certificate`     | Closeout      | P3       |
| `punch-item.schema.json`           | `PunchItem`           | Closeout      | P2       |
| `commissioning-system.schema.json` | `CommissioningSystem` | Commissioning | P3       |
| `functional-test.schema.json`      | `FunctionalTest`      | Commissioning | P3       |
| `cx-issue.schema.json`             | `CxIssue`             | Commissioning | P3       |
| `warranty.schema.json`             | `Warranty`            | Warranty      | P3       |
| `warranty-claim.schema.json`       | `WarrantyClaim`       | Warranty      | P3       |
| `building-history.schema.json`     | `BuildingHistory`     | Warranty      | P3       |

### 2.3 Model Implementation Order

**Phase A1: Contract Domain (Week 1) - Blocking for Authority Cascade**

```
1. Contract model (depends on: Project)
2. ContractParty model (depends on: Contract)
3. ContractTemplate model (standalone)
4. Update Project model with Contract relation
```

**Phase A2: IPD Governance (Week 2) - Blocking for Closeout**

```
5. VotingSession model (depends on: Project)
6. TargetCost model (depends on: Project)
7. SavingsDistribution model (depends on: Closeout - circular, resolve with nullable)
```

**Phase A3: Closeout Domain (Week 3)**

```
8. Closeout model (depends on: Project, Contract)
9. G704Certificate model (depends on: Closeout)
10. PunchItem model (depends on: Closeout, Voxel)
```

**Phase A4: Commissioning Domain (Week 4)**

```
11. CommissioningSystem model (depends on: Project)
12. FunctionalTest model (depends on: CommissioningSystem)
13. CxIssue model (depends on: CommissioningSystem, PMDecision link)
```

**Phase A5: Warranty Domain (Week 5)**

```
14. Warranty model (depends on: Project)
15. WarrantyClaim model (depends on: Warranty, PMDecision link)
16. BuildingHistory model (depends on: Project) - Query cache
```

### 2.4 Validation Checkpoints

After each phase:

```bash
# Generate Prisma client
pnpm prisma generate

# Create migration (review before applying!)
pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_{domain}_models

# Verify TypeScript compilation
pnpm nx build mcp-server

# Run existing tests
pnpm nx test mcp-server
```

---

## PART 3: WORKSTREAM B - GRAPH MIGRATION PREPARATION

### 3.1 Current Graph-Ready Architecture

All V3 entities include `graphMetadata` JSONB column:

```json
{
  "inEdges": ["urn:luhtech:project-alpha:voxel:VOX-L2-MECH-047"],
  "outEdges": ["urn:luhtech:project-alpha:consequence:CONSQ-2026-0001"]
}
```

This enables dual-write without schema changes.

### 3.2 Phase 1: Dual-Write Infrastructure (40 hours)

**Goal:** Write graph relationships to both PostgreSQL JSONB and Neo4j simultaneously.

**Tasks:**

1. **Neo4j Service Setup**

   ```yaml
   # docker-compose.dev.yml addition
   neo4j:
     image: neo4j:5.15-community
     ports:
       - '7474:7474' # Browser
       - '7687:7687' # Bolt
     environment:
       NEO4J_AUTH: neo4j/ectropy-graph-2026
     volumes:
       - neo4j_data:/data
   ```

2. **Graph Client Library**

   ```
   libs/graph-client/
   ├── src/
   │   ├── neo4j-client.ts      # Connection pool, Cypher execution
   │   ├── graph-sync.service.ts # Dual-write orchestration
   │   ├── urn-resolver.ts      # URN to Neo4j node mapping
   │   └── types.ts             # Node/Edge TypeScript types
   └── package.json
   ```

3. **Sync Service Integration**
   - Hook into Prisma `$extends` middleware
   - On create/update of entities with `graphMetadata`:
     - Parse inEdges/outEdges
     - Create/update Neo4j nodes and relationships
     - Log sync status for monitoring

4. **Graph Schema (Neo4j)**

   ```cypher
   // Node labels from V3 nodeTypes
   CREATE CONSTRAINT unique_urn FOR (n:Entity) REQUIRE n.urn IS UNIQUE;

   // Core node types
   (:Project {urn, name, status})
   (:Voxel {urn, voxelId, x, y, z, level, zone})
   (:PMDecision {urn, decisionId, type, status, authorityRequired})
   (:Participant {urn, participantId, name, authorityLevel})
   (:Consequence {urn, consequenceId, category, severity})
   (:Inspection {urn, inspectionId, type, status})
   (:Contract {urn, contractId, type})
   (:Closeout {urn, status})
   (:CommissioningSystem {urn, systemType, status})
   (:Warranty {urn, warrantyType, endDate})

   // Edge types (from pm-decision edges + new)
   -[:CONTAINS]->        // Project contains Voxel
   -[:ATTACHED_TO]->     // PMDecision attached to Voxel
   -[:TRIGGERS]->        // PMDecision triggers Consequence
   -[:ESCALATES_TO]->    // PMDecision escalates to PMDecision
   -[:AUTHORED_BY]->     // PMDecision authored by Participant
   -[:APPROVED_BY]->     // PMDecision approved by Participant
   -[:VALIDATES]->       // Inspection validates PMDecision
   -[:GOVERNS]->         // Contract governs Project
   -[:ROOT_CAUSE]->      // CxIssue root cause PMDecision
   -[:WARRANTS]->        // Warranty warrants CommissioningSystem
   ```

### 3.3 Phase 2: Graph-First Queries (80 hours) - Post-Pilot

**Goal:** Route relationship-heavy queries to Neo4j while keeping CRUD in PostgreSQL.

**Key Queries to Migrate:**

| Query                      | Current Method          | Graph Method        |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------- |
| Root cause analysis        | 5+ JOINs, recursive CTE | `MATCH path=...`    |
| Decision impact preview    | Application code        | Graph projection    |
| Authority chain traversal  | Multiple queries        | Single Cypher       |
| Spatial neighbor decisions | PostGIS + JOIN          | Cypher with spatial |

**Not for Phase 2:**

- User/Project CRUD (stays in PostgreSQL)
- File uploads (stays in PostgreSQL)
- Audit logs (stays in PostgreSQL)

### 3.4 Graph Migration Validation

```bash
# Verify dual-write parity
pnpm nx run graph-client:validate-sync --project=demo-project

# Query performance comparison
pnpm nx run graph-client:benchmark --query=root-cause-analysis

# Data integrity check
pnpm nx run graph-client:check-orphans
```

---

## PART 4: WORKSTREAM C - SEPPA MCP OPERATIONALIZATION

### 4.1 Tool Registration Process

For each new tool, follow this pattern:

**Step 1: Create Tool Handler in pm-decision-tools.ts (or new module)**

```typescript
// apps/mcp-server/src/services/contract-tools.ts (NEW FILE)
export const contractTools = [
  {
    name: 'parse_contract_tool',
    description:
      'Parse a contract document and extract key terms, authority levels, and governance rules',
    inputSchema: {
      type: 'object',
      required: ['documentUri', 'contractType'],
      properties: {
        documentUri: {
          type: 'string',
          description: 'URI to contract document (PDF or DOCX)',
        },
        contractType: {
          type: 'string',
          enum: [
            'STIPULATED_SUM',
            'COST_PLUS_FEE',
            'GMP',
            'IPD',
            'DESIGN_BUILD',
          ],
          description: 'Expected contract type for parsing strategy',
        },
        extractSections: {
          type: 'array',
          items: { type: 'string' },
          description:
            'Specific sections to extract (e.g., ["authority", "changes", "disputes"])',
        },
      },
    },
    handler: async (input: ParseContractInput, context: ToolContext) => {
      // Implementation calls contract-parser-service
    },
  },
  // ... more contract tools
];
```

**Step 2: Register in tool-registry.ts**

```typescript
import { contractTools } from '../contract-tools.js';
import { ipdTools } from '../ipd-tools.js';
import { closeoutTools } from '../closeout-tools.js';
// ... etc

export const toolRegistry: ClaudeTool[] = [
  ...coreTools,
  ...pmTools,
  ...contractTools.map(convertToClaudeTool),
  ...ipdTools.map(convertToClaudeTool),
  ...closeoutTools.map(convertToClaudeTool),
  // ...
];
```

**Step 3: Implement Tool Executor Handler**

```typescript
// apps/mcp-server/src/services/assistant/tool-executor.ts
// Add case for new tool category
case 'parse_contract_tool':
  return await this.contractService.parseContract(toolInput);
```

**Step 4: Update System Prompt**

```typescript
// apps/mcp-server/src/services/assistant/system-prompt.ts
// Add tool category guidance
const toolGuidance = `
## Contract Management Tools
Use these when the user asks about:
- Contract terms, authority levels, or governance rules
- IPD profit-at-risk or savings distribution
- Who can approve what dollar amount

Tools: parse_contract_tool, extract_authority_cascade_tool, configure_project_from_contract_tool
`;
```

### 4.2 Tool Implementation Phases

**Phase C1: Contract Tools (Week 2-3) - 3 Tools**

| Tool                                   | Prisma Models Needed              | External Dependencies           |
| -------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| `parse_contract_tool`                  | Contract, ContractParty           | PDF extraction (pdf-parse), NLP |
| `extract_authority_cascade_tool`       | Contract, AuthorityLevel          | Rule engine                     |
| `configure_project_from_contract_tool` | Contract, Project, AuthorityLevel | None                            |

**Phase C2: IPD Governance Tools (Week 3-4) - 6 Tools**

| Tool                                | Prisma Models Needed            | External Dependencies |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| `initiate_pmt_vote_tool`            | VotingSession                   | None                  |
| `cast_vote_tool`                    | VotingSession                   | None                  |
| `get_voting_status_tool`            | VotingSession                   | None                  |
| `escalate_to_pet_tool`              | VotingSession                   | Notification service  |
| `update_target_cost_tool`           | TargetCost, VotingSession       | None                  |
| `calculate_savings_projection_tool` | TargetCost, SavingsDistribution | Financial calculation |

**Phase C3: Closeout Tools (Week 4-5) - 3 Tools**

| Tool                                   | Prisma Models Needed            | External Dependencies    |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `initiate_closeout_sequence_tool`      | Closeout, CommissioningSystem   | None                     |
| `generate_substantial_completion_tool` | G704Certificate, PunchItem      | PDF generation (pdf-lib) |
| `generate_final_accounting_tool`       | SavingsDistribution, TargetCost | Financial calculation    |

**Phase C4: Commissioning Tools (Week 5-6) - 3 Tools**

| Tool                             | Prisma Models Needed                | External Dependencies |
| -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| `log_fpt_result_tool`            | FunctionalTest, CommissioningSystem | None                  |
| `link_cx_issue_to_decision_tool` | CxIssue, PMDecision                 | Graph linkage         |
| `generate_cx_documentation_tool` | CommissioningSystem, FunctionalTest | PDF generation        |

**Phase C5: Punch List Tools (Week 6-7) - 4 Tools**

| Tool                             | Prisma Models Needed   | External Dependencies |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------- | --------------------- |
| `create_punch_item_tool`         | PunchItem, Voxel       | Voice note processing |
| `update_punch_item_tool`         | PunchItem              | None                  |
| `assign_punch_item_tool`         | PunchItem, Participant | Twilio SMS            |
| `validate_punch_completion_tool` | PunchItem              | GPS validation        |

**Phase C6: Warranty/Operations Tools (Week 7-8) - 3 Tools**

| Tool                           | Prisma Models Needed    | External Dependencies         |
| ------------------------------ | ----------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| `create_warranty_claim_tool`   | WarrantyClaim, Warranty | Vendor notification           |
| `search_decision_archive_tool` | All decision models     | Full-text search (PostgreSQL) |
| `query_building_history_tool`  | BuildingHistory         | RAG layer (Phase 5), Claude   |

After all phases complete:


### 4.4 Frontend Integration (Already Specified in SEPPA Handoff)

The MCPChatPanel.tsx update is defined in the SEPPA_CLAUDE_INTEGRATION_HANDOFF.md document. Key changes:

1. Switch from keyword-based to POST `/api/assistant/chat`
2. Add streaming response handling (SSE)
3. Show tool execution chips
4. Maintain conversation context

## PART 5: IMPLEMENTATION PROMPTS

### Prompt A1: Prisma Contract Domain Models

```
CONTEXT:
You are implementing the Contract domain Prisma models for Ectropy. The V3 JSON schemas are complete in `.roadmap/schemas/contract/`. The existing schema.prisma has 12 Decision Lifecycle models that follow V3 patterns with URN identifiers and graphMetadata JSONB columns.

TASK:
Create Prisma models for Contract, ContractParty, and ContractTemplate. Add required enums. Update Project model with Contract relation.

SOURCE FILES:
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract-party.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract-template.schema.json
- prisma/schema.prisma (existing patterns to follow)

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
1. All models have: id (UUID), urn (unique), graphMetadata (JSONB), created_at, updated_at
2. URN pattern: urn:luhtech:{project}:contract:{contractId}
3. Map snake_case to camelCase with @map()
4. Add appropriate indexes for common queries
5. Include JSDoc comments for complex fields

VALIDATION:
- pnpm prisma generate (no errors)
- pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_contract_models --create-only (review SQL)
- pnpm nx build mcp-server

OUTPUT:
- Updated prisma/schema.prisma with Contract domain models
- Migration file created (not applied - needs review)
```

### Prompt A2: Prisma IPD Governance Models

```
CONTEXT:
Contract domain models are complete. Now implementing IPD Governance models. These models support multi-party voting (PMT/PET), Target Cost management, and savings distribution calculation.

TASK:
Create Prisma models for VotingSession, TargetCost, and SavingsDistribution. Note: SavingsDistribution references Closeout which may not exist yet - use optional relation.

SOURCE FILES:
- .roadmap/schemas/ipd/voting-session.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/ipd/target-cost.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/ipd/savings-distribution.schema.json

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
1. VotingSession.votes is JSONB array of vote objects (not separate table)
2. TargetCost has versioning (version field + status enum)
3. SavingsDistribution.distributions is JSONB array per party

VALIDATION:
- pnpm prisma generate
- pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_ipd_models --create-only
- pnpm nx build mcp-server
```

### Prompt A3: Prisma Closeout Domain Models

```
CONTEXT:
Contract and IPD models are complete. Now implementing Closeout domain models for project completion workflow, G704 certificates, and punch list management.

TASK:
Create Prisma models for Closeout, G704Certificate, and PunchItem. Update SavingsDistribution to reference Closeout (now exists). Add Voxel relation to PunchItem.

SOURCE FILES:
- .roadmap/schemas/closeout/closeout.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/closeout/g704-certificate.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/closeout/punch-item.schema.json

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
1. G704Certificate is 1:1 with Closeout
2. PunchItem has voxel attachment for spatial context
3. PunchItem.completion_photos is JSONB array

VALIDATION:
- pnpm prisma generate
- pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_closeout_models --create-only
- pnpm nx build mcp-server
```

### Prompt B1: Neo4j Dual-Write Service

```
CONTEXT:
All 27 Prisma models exist with graphMetadata JSONB columns. Need to implement dual-write to Neo4j for graph query preparation.

TASK:
Create graph-client library with Neo4j connection, sync service, and URN resolver.

FILES TO CREATE:
libs/graph-client/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Barrel exports
│   ├── neo4j-client.ts       # Connection pool, Cypher execution
│   ├── graph-sync.service.ts # Dual-write orchestration
│   ├── urn-resolver.ts       # URN to Neo4j node mapping
│   └── types.ts              # Node/Edge TypeScript types
├── project.json              # NX configuration
└── package.json

PATTERNS:
1. Use neo4j-driver npm package
2. Connection pool with max 50 sessions
3. Retry logic for transient failures
4. Logging with structured format

INTEGRATION:
- Hook into Prisma middleware in mcp-server
- Fire-and-forget async (don't block PostgreSQL writes)
- Log sync failures for retry queue

VALIDATION:
- Docker Neo4j container running
- pnpm nx build graph-client
- Manual test: Create PMDecision, verify Neo4j node created
```

## PART 6: SUCCESS CRITERIA

### Canadian Pilot Ready (March 2026)

| Requirement                        | Validation                                   |
| ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| All 15 Prisma models migrated      | `pnpm prisma migrate status` shows applied   |
| All 39 MCP tools registered        | `getToolRegistrySummary().total === 39`      |
| Neo4j dual-write active            | Graph contains all PMDecision nodes          |
| SEPPA responds to contract queries | "What's my authority limit?" returns cascade |
| Punch list SMS workflow works      | Field worker can complete item via text      |

### Metrics Targets

| Metric                 | Target                      | Measurement                 |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Tool call success rate | >95%                        | Logging in tool-executor    |
| SEPPA response time    | <5s simple, <15s multi-tool | Request timing              |
| Graph sync latency     | <100ms                      | Neo4j write timestamp delta |
| Model coverage         | 27/27 models                | Prisma schema count         |

sections.executive_summary:
This handoff document provides everything needed to continue Ectropy development across three interconnected workstreams:

1. **Prisma Model Extension** - Add 15 Contract-to-Operations models to existing schema
2. **Graph Migration Preparation** - Dual-write architecture for PostgreSQL → Neo4j
3. **SEPPA MCP Operationalization** - Register 22 new tools, connect to Claude assistant

**Critical Dependencies:**

- Phase 3-4 Contract-to-Operations documentation is COMPLETE (39 MCP tools specified)
- SEPPA backend (Phase 3) is COMPLETE with 24 tools registered
- All V3 JSON schemas exist in `.roadmap/schemas/`
- Repository split scheduled for January 17, 2026

---

sections.3_1_current_graph_ready_architecture:
All V3 entities include `graphMetadata` JSONB column:

```json
{
  "inEdges": ["urn:luhtech:project-alpha:voxel:VOX-L2-MECH-047"],
  "outEdges": ["urn:luhtech:project-alpha:consequence:CONSQ-2026-0001"]
}
```

This enables dual-write without schema changes.
sections.3_2_phase_1_dual_write_infrastructure_40:
**Goal:** Write graph relationships to both PostgreSQL JSONB and Neo4j simultaneously.

**Tasks:**

1. **Neo4j Service Setup**

   ```yaml
   # docker-compose.dev.yml addition
   neo4j:
     image: neo4j:5.15-community
     ports:
       - '7474:7474' # Browser
       - '7687:7687' # Bolt
     environment:
       NEO4J_AUTH: neo4j/ectropy-graph-2026
     volumes:
       - neo4j_data:/data
   ```

2. **Graph Client Library**

   ```
   libs/graph-client/
   ├── src/
   │   ├── neo4j-client.ts      # Connection pool, Cypher execution
   │   ├── graph-sync.service.ts # Dual-write orchestration
   │   ├── urn-resolver.ts      # URN to Neo4j node mapping
   │   └── types.ts             # Node/Edge TypeScript types
   └── package.json
   ```

3. **Sync Service Integration**
   - Hook into Prisma `$extends` middleware
   - On create/update of entities with `graphMetadata`:
     - Parse inEdges/outEdges
     - Create/update Neo4j nodes and relationships
     - Log sync status for monitoring

4. **Graph Schema (Neo4j)**

   ```cypher
   // Node labels from V3 nodeTypes
   CREATE CONSTRAINT unique_urn FOR (n:Entity) REQUIRE n.urn IS UNIQUE;

   // Core node types
   (:Project {urn, name, status})
   (:Voxel {urn, voxelId, x, y, z, level, zone})
   (:PMDecision {urn, decisionId, type, status, authorityRequired})
   (:Participant {urn, participantId, name, authorityLevel})
   (:Consequence {urn, consequenceId, category, severity})
   (:Inspection {urn, inspectionId, type, status})
   (:Contract {urn, contractId, type})
   (:Closeout {urn, status})
   (:CommissioningSystem {urn, systemType, status})
   (:Warranty {urn, warrantyType, endDate})

   // Edge types (from pm-decision edges + new)
   -[:CONTAINS]->        // Project contains Voxel
   -[:ATTACHED_TO]->     // PMDecision attached to Voxel
   -[:TRIGGERS]->        // PMDecision triggers Consequence
   -[:ESCALATES_TO]->    // PMDecision escalates to PMDecision
   -[:AUTHORED_BY]->     // PMDecision authored by Participant
   -[:APPROVED_BY]->     // PMDecision approved by Participant
   -[:VALIDATES]->       // Inspection validates PMDecision
   -[:GOVERNS]->         // Contract governs Project
   -[:ROOT_CAUSE]->      // CxIssue root cause PMDecision
   -[:WARRANTS]->        // Warranty warrants CommissioningSystem
   ```
sections.3_3_phase_2_graph_first_queries_80_hours:
**Goal:** Route relationship-heavy queries to Neo4j while keeping CRUD in PostgreSQL.

**Key Queries to Migrate:**

| Query                      | Current Method          | Graph Method        |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------- |
| Root cause analysis        | 5+ JOINs, recursive CTE | `MATCH path=...`    |
| Decision impact preview    | Application code        | Graph projection    |
| Authority chain traversal  | Multiple queries        | Single Cypher       |
| Spatial neighbor decisions | PostGIS + JOIN          | Cypher with spatial |

**Not for Phase 2:**

- User/Project CRUD (stays in PostgreSQL)
- File uploads (stays in PostgreSQL)
- Audit logs (stays in PostgreSQL)

```

sections.prompt_a1_prisma_contract_domain_models:
```
CONTEXT:
You are implementing the Contract domain Prisma models for Ectropy. The V3 JSON schemas are complete in `.roadmap/schemas/contract/`. The existing schema.prisma has 12 Decision Lifecycle models that follow V3 patterns with URN identifiers and graphMetadata JSONB columns.

TASK:
Create Prisma models for Contract, ContractParty, and ContractTemplate. Add required enums. Update Project model with Contract relation.

SOURCE FILES:
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract-party.schema.json
- .roadmap/schemas/contract/contract-template.schema.json
- prisma/schema.prisma (existing patterns to follow)

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
1. All models have: id (UUID), urn (unique), graphMetadata (JSONB), created_at, updated_at
2. URN pattern: urn:luhtech:{project}:contract:{contractId}
3. Map snake_case to camelCase with @map()
4. Add appropriate indexes for common queries
5. Include JSDoc comments for complex fields

VALIDATION:
- pnpm prisma generate (no errors)
- pnpm prisma migrate dev --name add_contract_models --create-only (review SQL)
- pnpm nx build mcp-server

OUTPUT:
- Updated prisma/schema.prisma with Contract domain models
- Migration file created (not applied - needs review)

├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Barrel exports
│   ├── neo4j-client.ts       # Connection pool, Cypher execution
│   ├── graph-sync.service.ts # Dual-write orchestration
│   ├── urn-resolver.ts       # URN to Neo4j node mapping
│   └── types.ts              # Node/Edge TypeScript types
├── project.json              # NX configuration
└── package.json

PATTERNS:
1. Use neo4j-driver npm package
2. Connection pool with max 50 sessions
3. Retry logic for transient failures
4. Logging with structured format

INTEGRATION:
- Hook into Prisma middleware in mcp-server
- Fire-and-forget async (don't block PostgreSQL writes)
- Log sync failures for retry queue

VALIDATION:
- Docker Neo4j container running
- pnpm nx build graph-client
- Manual test: Create PMDecision, verify Neo4j node created

Contract tools are implemented. Now adding IPD Governance tools for voting and target cost management.

TASK:
Implement 6 IPD Governance tools: initiate_pmt_vote_tool, cast_vote_tool, get_voting_status_tool, escalate_to_pet_tool, update_target_cost_tool, calculate_savings_projection_tool.

SOURCE SPECIFICATION:

- apps/mcp-server/src/services/assistant/tool-registry.ts (ADD imports)
- apps/mcp-server/src/services/assistant/tool-executor.ts (ADD cases)

SPECIAL LOGIC:
- initiate_pmt_vote_tool: Create VotingSession, notify members
- escalate_to_pet_tool: Copy PMT session to PET level
- calculate_savings_projection_tool: Financial formula implementation

VALIDATION:

- Test via /api/assistant/chat: "Start a PMT vote on increasing contingency"

```
sections.prompt_c3_seppa_rag_integration_phase_5_:
```
CONTEXT:
MCP tools are operational. For query_building_history_tool to work properly, need RAG layer for document retrieval.

TASK:
Set up RAG service foundation with Qdrant and Jina embeddings.

FILES TO CREATE:
apps/rag-service/
├── src/
│   ├── main.ts               # Express server on port 4006
│   ├── routes/
│   │   └── search.routes.ts  # Vector search endpoints
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── qdrant.service.ts # Qdrant client
│   │   ├── embedding.service.ts # Jina embeddings
│   │   └── indexer.service.ts # Document chunking and indexing
│   └── types.ts
├── project.json
└── package.json

DOCKER:
- Add Qdrant service to docker-compose
- Configure collection for project_documents

INTEGRATION:
- query_building_history_tool calls rag-service for context
- Context assembled with token budget (4000 tokens max)
- Citations returned with document URNs

VALIDATION:
- pnpm nx build rag-service
- Index test document
- Query and verify relevant chunks returned
